steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The claim requires both direct reader address and establishment of authority/credibility. Since all options directly address the reader (“dear reader” or similar), the key signal is: which quotation explicitly asserts Winnemucca's credibility to speak?

steps.title: Confirm the explicit claim
steps.content: Choice D directly states “I know what I am talking about”—an explicit assertion of credibility rooted in her lived experience. Choices A, B, and C instead appeal to imagination, demonstrate cultural knowledge, or explain a tradition—none makes an explicit claim to authority. That matches choice D — D is correct.
